hypothesis: Using a soft, classifier-weighted mixture of symmetry projection operators—where each gradient step in XRD-based optimization is projected onto a weighted sum of crystal-family projectors with weights given by a space-group posterior from an XRD-based classifier—will yield higher StructureMatcher recovery rates and reduced incidence of local-minimum trapping than both unconstrained optimization and hard single-space-group projection when reconstructing structures from moderately distorted XRD patterns.
